facts.text: Jackson Heights was named after John Jackson, president of Hunters Point and Flushing Turnpike Company, who operated the Jackson Avenue trolley.
The neighborhood was planned by the Queensboro Corporation and dedicated in 1923, with over 200 original apartment buildings still standing.
Northern Boulevard, the main east‑west artery, was originally called Jackson Avenue and still keeps that name in a short stretch at its western terminus.
In 1993, the first Queens Pride Parade was held in Jackson Heights, marking a turning point in the borough’s social history.
The Jackson Heights Beautification Group, founded in 1988, organized walking tours and helped commission a neighborhood history that now guides local preservation.
The area that became Jackson Heights was once a vast marsh known as Trains Meadow before developers drained it in the early 1900s.
